description: Safe math evaluation and JSON Schema validation.

tools.description:
Evaluates arithmetic expressions exactly or computes summary statistics over a list of numbers.

When to use: Use whenever the user asks for any calculation, total, average, percentage, percentile, standard deviation, or numeric comparison. Never do arithmetic in the model head — call this tool. Supports `eval` mode for expressions and `stats` mode for descriptive statistics.

How to communicate: Return the numeric result in plain prose with units if known; for `stats` give a short labelled summary (mean, median, std, p95) instead of dumping the raw object.

Example: user asks 'what's monthly payment on 1500 at 8% over 12 months' -> call {mode:'eval', expression:'(1500 * 1.08) / 12'}. For 'std dev of these scores [82,90,75,88,91]' -> call {mode:'stats', values:'[82,90,75,88,91]'}.

schema.name: mode
schema.description: Which calculation path to take. Use `eval` to evaluate a single arithmetic expression, or `stats` to compute mean/median/std/percentiles over a numeric list. Defaults to `eval` when omitted.

schema.name: expression
schema.description: Arithmetic expression to evaluate when `mode='eval'`. Supports `+ - * / // % **`, `sqrt`, `log`, `sin`, `abs`, `round`, `min`, `max`, `sum`, and constants `pi`, `e`. Max 4000 characters. Required when `mode='eval'`; ignored otherwise.

schema.name: values
schema.description: JSON array of numbers to summarize when `mode='stats'`. Must parse as a JSON list of numeric values, e.g. `[82, 90, 75, 88, 91]`. Required when `mode='stats'`; ignored otherwise.

_confDescriptions.max_expression_length: Max characters allowed in an arithmetic expression (eval mode). Guards against pathological inputs.
_confDescriptions.max_stats_values: Max number of values accepted in stats mode. Caps memory/CPU for percentile + std computations.

tools.description:
Validates a data object against a JSON Schema and returns any violations.

When to use: Use as a quality gate before acting on structured data — verify an API response, a tool result, or user-supplied JSON has the expected shape before passing it downstream. Also use when the user explicitly asks to validate, check, or lint a JSON payload against a schema.

How to communicate: If valid, say so in one line. If invalid, list each violation as `path: message` (e.g. `/items/0/price: must be number`); do not dump the full schema or data back to the user.

Example: user says 'does this response match my schema' -> call {data:'{"name":"x","age":"30"}', schema:'{"type":"object","properties":{"age":{"type":"integer"}}}', mode:'best_effort'} -> report the type mismatch on /age.

schema.name: data
schema.description: The payload to validate. Accepts a JSON string or a JSON-serializable object/array, e.g. `{"name":"Ada","age":36}` or `[1,2,3]`. Required.

schema.name: schema
schema.description: A JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) describing the expected shape of `data`. Pass as a JSON string, e.g. `{"type":"object","required":["id"],"properties":{"id":{"type":"integer"}}}`. Required.

schema.name: mode
schema.description: Validation strategy. Use `best_effort` to collect every violation (best for reporting to the user). Use `fail_fast` to stop at the first violation when you only need a pass/fail signal. Defaults to `best_effort` when omitted.

_confDescriptions.max_violations: Max number of schema violations returned in best_effort mode. Prevents oversized error payloads.
